events.title: Poland strips Zelensky of top state honour over WWII dispute
events.description: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Poland strips Zelensky of top state honour over WWII dispute

events.title: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
events.description: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan

events.title: Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians
events.description: Colombian military forces neutralized five members of the militant group allegedly responsible for the bus bombing that killed 20 civilians in Cauca.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians

events.title: World Refugee Day: UN calls for renewed commitment and solidarity
events.description: The UN High Commissioner for Refugees has called on the international community to strengthen support for the nearly 42 million people worldwide who have fled their home countries to escape conflict, violence or persecution.

events.title: Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk
events.description: Abdiqadir Salah was pierced by shrapnel in a bombing that killed 12 in Somalia. But as the US denies civilians were hurt they face no hope of compensation Read more: Killed walking home from school: why did Somali children become targets of US drone strikes? A seven-year-old boy who was riddled with shrapnel during a deadly US airstrike in Somalia faces losing his ability to walk unless he has a £750 emergency operation. But Abdiqadir Salah’s family cannot afford the surgery and the US – which refuses to admit that any civilians were killed or injured during its attack six months ago – appears unwilling to pay compensation to those affected by airstrikes in Somalia. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk

events.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
events.description: The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean

events.title: Drone use expands in Sudan – and kills at least 1,000 civilians in 2026
events.description: According to the United Nations, more than 1,000 civilians have been killed in drone strikes in Sudan since the beginning of the year. There has been a sharp increase in the use of drone warfare – a tactic marking a turning point in the three-year-war, which has created what the UN deems the "world's worst humanitarian crisis." Details by FRANCE 24 regional correspondent Bastien Renouil.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone use expands in Sudan – and kills at least 1,000 civilians in 2026

events.title: NGOs in Lebanon denounce Israeli attacks on medics amid ceasefire
events.description: On March 2nd, Hezbollah dragged Lebanon into the war when it launched rockets at Israel in what it said was revenge for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader. Since then, Israel's campaign has killed more than 3,900 people and displaced 20% of the population. The humanitarian situation on the ground is dire. Details from the ground with Dr Mina Naguib, an emergency physician with Doctors Without Borders in Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NGOs in Lebanon denounce Israeli attacks on medics amid ceasefire

events.title: Could Israel lose Donald Trump’s support by continuing to drop bombs in Lebanon?
events.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least seven people, including two children, hours after reports emerged of a ceasefire agreement. The persistent fighting threatened an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to end the war in the Middle East. Could Israel' Netanyahu lose Trump's support if strikes continue? Analysis by Yossi Mekelberg, senior consulting fellow at MENAP, Chatam House, London.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Could Israel lose Donald Trump’s support by continuing to drop bombs in Lebanon?

events.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire after continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon
events.description: Iran has closed the strait of Hormuz – citing alleged ⁠violations of a ceasefire agreement ​by the US ‌and Israel. Tehran said the closure was the "first step" in response ​to what it described ​as breaches ​of commitments regarding the continuing clashes in Lebanon - and warned ​that further measures would be taken if "aggression" continued. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani, and FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran closes Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire after continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon

events.title: Two-train crash leaves at least 1 dead, 89 injured as emergency crews rush to chaotic scene
events.description: Two trains crashed into each other near Bedford, England, with an ambulance service confirming at least 1 death and over 80 people injured.

events.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
events.description: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the world. The officials said the outbreak, now the third largest on record, required “strong immediate support”, but that the global risk remained low. Continue reading...

events.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
events.description: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ design Canadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft. The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the Atlantic Ocean in June 2023 en route to the wreckage of the Titanic ocean liner. But nearly two hours after it departed with five passengers, communications went dark. The disappearance prompted a frantic international search, with Canada and the US marshalling all available resources . Continue reading...

events.title: From ‘media deserts’ to the invisibility of women, rights experts spotlight latest trends
events.description: From the invisibility of women and girls to “media deserts” amid an artificial intelligence (AI) tsunami, dozens of UN independent experts have been shedding light on the battle for equal rights worldwide as the Geneva-based Human Rights Council continued its 62nd session on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ From ‘media deserts’ to the invisibility of women, rights experts spotlight latest trends

events.title: Hate speech is ‘first step down the path of dehumanisation’
events.description: As online platforms continue to fuel a surge in real-world violence against vulnerable communities, UN Secretary-General António Guterres is warning that freedom of expression must never be used to justify hate speech.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hate speech is ‘first step down the path of dehumanisation’

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
events.description: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly 1,000 Palestinians have been killed, and most Gazans remain displaced. Relief chief Tom Fletcher told ambassadors that “fragile gains” since the truce are “the bare minimum of what Palestinians need.” Full coverage here, and wider Meetings Coverage can be found here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue

events.title: Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings
events.description: After another deadly night of clashes in Lebanon, aid agencies issued a new alert for Gaza, where 265 Palestinian children have been killed since a ceasefire was announced in October 2025.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings

events.title: How cities are adapting to a warming world
events.description: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How cities are adapting to a warming world

events.title: Europe swelters in gruelling heatwave, authorities ramp up warnings
events.description: A severe heatwave is sweeping across much of Europe, prompting emergency meetings in France, nationwide warnings in Germany and strains on tourists and residents ​in Italy. Temperatures climbed to record levels, and are set to hit 40°C (105.0 °F). Details by Shirli Sitbon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe swelters in gruelling heatwave, authorities ramp up warnings

events.title: Worsening hunger could push millions closer to famine in 13 global hotspots
events.description: Millions of people across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions are expected to face worsening hunger in the months ahead, as conflict, economic pressures and shrinking aid budgets exacerbate long-running crises.

events.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
events.description: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery

events.title: Germany news: Summer thunderstorms wreak havoc
events.description: Germany was lashed overnight by summer storms that caused flooding, injuries and evacuations. Lightning, high winds and heavy rain saw several people hospitalized. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Germany news: Summer thunderstorms wreak havoc

events.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
events.description: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century

events.title: Fears doctors criticising Israel may be silenced as health watchdog adopts contested antisemitism definition
events.description: Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency to use International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance definition for ‘regulatory work’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s health watchdog has adopted a contested definition of antisemitism to guide its regulatory work. The move has been welcomed by the nation’s peak Jewish body while the Australia Palestine Advocacy Network (Apan) has warned it could silence health professionals from criticising Israel’s human rights record. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears doctors criticising Israel may be silenced as health watchdog adopts contested antisemitism definition

events.title: Israel strikes Lebanon despite ceasefire, at least 16 killed
events.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed at least 16 people on Saturday, Lebanese Civil Defencesaid, hours after a ceasefire with Hezbollah suposedly took effect. ​Israel says it ‌was responding to projectiles fired by the Iran-backed group. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondant in Jerusalem, Noga Tarnopolsky.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel strikes Lebanon despite ceasefire, at least 16 killed

events.title: Bolivia's Paz declares state of emergency over blockades
events.description: President Rodrigo Paz says he has declared the state of emergency "to free the country's roads." Blockades have become a major tactic in weeks of intensifying protests to demand that the president resign.

events.title: Climate shocks accelerating as El Niño threat looms over already vulnerable regions
events.description: Millions of people already facing hunger, displacement and economic hardship could soon face another major climate shock, as UN agencies warned on Thursday that extreme weather risks are intensifying across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Climate shocks accelerating as El Niño threat looms over already vulnerable regions

events.title: DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders
events.description: The spread of Ebola is accelerating in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) amid continued armed violence, posing a grave and growing risk to the region, UN agencies warned on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders

events.title: NYPD officer shot, gunman killed after armed barricaded standoff in Brooklyn: police
events.description: An NYPD ESU detective was shot in the leg during a Brooklyn standoff with an armed man who allegedly fired 20 rounds before being fatally struck.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 NYPD officer shot, gunman killed after armed barricaded standoff in Brooklyn: police

events.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
events.description: At least 20 people have reportedly been killed by Israeli air strikes as the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement

events.title: UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability
events.description: The United Nations has welcomed reports of a new ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah on Friday while warning that civilians on the ground are still fleeing amid ongoing insecurity.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability

events.title: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
events.description: The head of the UN-affiliated atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency

events.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference
events.description: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmap More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: At least 24 killed in Kashmir as protests spark shutdown
events.description: At least 24 people in Pakistan-administered Kashmir have been killed in nearly two weeks of protests that have sparked a territory-wide shutdown.

events.title: Nigerian migrants returning from South Africa face the same economic issues they left behind
events.description: Nigerians repatriated from South Africa following a series of anti-migrant marches and reported attacks on some foreign nationals are struggling to settle into a new life back home

events.title: 'Pure hell' in Moscow as Ukrainian drones strike major refinery supplying capital's fuel market
events.description: Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks on Moscow, striking a major oil refinery and sending thick black smoke over parts of the Russian capital.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Pure hell' in Moscow as Ukrainian drones strike major refinery supplying capital's fuel market

events.title: In Nepal’s highlands, climate change threatens Tibet’s Bon faith
events.description: In Lubra, Nepal’s last Bon village, climate-driven floods are destroying homes and land, endangering ancient traditions.

events.title: Spain vs Saudi Arabia: World Cup 2026 – Yamal, prediction, watch, kickoff
events.description: Spain face Saudi Arabia in need of a win to take control of Group H. Can they rediscover their attacking edge?

events.title: What the US-Iran peace deal means for the ‘Team Melli’ World Cup campaign
events.description: Experts hope the 'extra animosity' towards Team Melli will subside as the US-Iran agreement comes into being.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What the US-Iran peace deal means for the ‘Team Melli’ World Cup campaign

events.title: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds
events.description: Review reveals rise in users and rates of psychosis in countries where cannabis is sold commercially Decriminalising the possession of cannabis or strictly regulating access to the drug do not appear to drive up usage, but when the drug is sold commercially the number of users increases and more mental health problems are seen, a review has found. An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug, its potency, and rates of psychosis changed after new rules came in. Continue reading...

events.title: With World Cup in Guadalajara, families of Mexico's disappeared turn loved ones into soccer stickers
events.description: Families in Guadalajara are using World Cup-themed posters to highlight Mexico's 135,000 missing people

events.title: ‘Darkest chapter’: Record child violations in 2025, with national forces leading the way
events.description: For the first time, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups – and 2025 set a grim new record for the total number of child victims.

events.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
events.description: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

events.title: MSF says conditions in Lebanon’s Nabatieh resemble a ‘death trap’
events.description: Israel has stepped up attacks on parts of southern Lebanon, including Nabatieh, since midnight on Friday.

events.title: Protesters to rally against World Cup sponsor Hyundai before Mexico game
events.description: Focus on business dealings with mining company Guadalajara rally to highlight fate of ‘disappeared’ Hyundai will be targeted by protesters at a rally before the Group A game between Mexico and South Korea in Guadalajara on Thursday, due to the World Cup sponsor’s business dealings with the South American mining company Ternium. A 2025 report from the environmental group Mighty Earth criticised Hyundai’s involvement in what they described as a “dirty steel supply chain”, as the South Korean motor company is a major buyer of iron ore from Ternium for use in steel production. Ternium has faced repeated criticisms for its destructive environmental impact and corporate governance policies from campaign groups, as well as its alleged links to the disappearance of two Mexican activists. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump says Italy's Meloni asked 'over and over' for a photo with him as diplomatic spat escalates
events.description: US President Donald Trump turned up the heat on a diplomatic row with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Saturday, accusing her of repeatedly seeking a photograph with him at the G7 summit and blaming the clash between the two heads of state on tensions over Iran and NATO. Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.

events.title: A boat with migrants capsized north off Libya, leaving 51 dead or missing, group says
events.description: A monitoring group says a boat carrying dozens of migrants bound for European shores capsized off the coast of Libya last week, leaving 51 people dead or missing

events.title: Karmelo Anthony runs away after murdering Austin Metcalf: surveillance video
events.description: Court officials in Texas have made public new images from the fatal scene after student Austin Metcalf was stabbed to death by Karmelo Anthony during a Texas high school track meet confrontation.

events.title: Russia linked to arson attacks on properties connected to UK PM Keir Starmer, police say
events.description: Russia allegedly linked to arson attacks on U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's properties, with a reported diplomat accused of recruiting Ukrainian nationals.

events.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
events.description: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
events.description: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia

events.title: KPMG leaked confidential Optus information and surveilled whistleblower’s laptop, inquiry hears
events.description: International firm owns up to breach of ethics after staff leaked confidential Optus information while bidding for telco contract Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast KPMG has admitted to another breach of ethics after its staff leaked Optus’ confidential information to colleagues bidding for an audit contract with Telstra. The consulting firm’s executives also surveilled a whistleblower’s laptop and dismissed the individual as someone with “workplace grievances”, a parliamentary inquiry heard on Friday. Continue reading...

events.title: Do Middle East nations still see the U.S. as a reliable ally?
events.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dania Thafer of the Gulf International Forum whether countries in the Middle East consider the U.S. a reliable ally.

events.title: African fashion takes centre stage at France's Galerie Lafayette
events.description: African fashion is no longer merely a source of inspiration for major international fashion houses. Today, it is establishing its own codes, its own brands and its own ambitions. To mark the ‘Africa Now’ event held at Paris’ Galeries Lafayette, FRANCE 24 welcoms a key figures in this movement: Adama N'Diaye, the Senegalese fashion designer behind Adama Paris.

events.title: Why Tunisia’s renewable energy strategy is facing resistance
events.description: Giving concessions for renewable projects to foreign corporations will not help solve the country’s energy crisis.

events.title: Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote
events.description: Colombians will choose on Sunday between two men whose lives have been very differently shaped by the militias, and whose visions for the country are poles apart Whoever wins Sunday’s presidential runoff vote in Colombia, the country’s next leader will have a personal history intertwined with one of the criminal forces at the heart of a decades-long armed conflict that claimed nearly half a million lives. The lives of Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella have, in very different ways, been shaped by their relationship with Colombia’s paramilitaries – private armies originally established by rightwing landowners, drug traffickers, businessmen, mining magnates and politicians to fight leftwing guerrilla groups. Continue reading...

events.title: Hezbollah-Israel flare-up in Lebanon despite ceasefire announced by the USA
events.description: US officials have announced that a ceasefire is in effect between Israel and Hezbollah. President Donald Trump claimed he had asked Israel for the ceasefire. However, it seems to be more on paper than reality: attacks and retaliation from both sides continued to flare following its theoretical coming into force on Friday afternoon local time. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondents in Jerusalem and Beirut, Noga Tarnopolski and Renée Davis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah-Israel flare-up in Lebanon despite ceasefire announced by the USA

events.title: Ebola outbreak kills 17 healthcare workers as Chinese medical team arrives in DRC
events.description: Seventeen healthcare workers have died from Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a World Health Organisation official has said.

events.title: About 300 children and teachers rescued after fire breaks out at Tokyo school
events.description: Officials say a fire broke out at an elementary school in downtown Tokyo, but that all of the roughly 300 students and teachers were evacuated or rescued

events.title: Texas woman faked pregnancy for months before killing friend, abducting her unborn baby
events.description: Taylor Parker, now on Texas death row, was convicted of capital murder after killing Reagan Hancock and abducting her unborn child back in 2020.

events.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
events.description: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

events.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
events.description: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring

events.title: Woman killed, hundreds evacuate in Dominican Republic hotel fire
events.description: A huge fire tore through a four-star beach resort in the Dominican Republic, killing one woman. About 1,700 tourists were evacuated as the fire appeared to destroy most of the hotel.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Team USA through to the round of 32 as Morocco and Brazil deliver
events.description: After Mexico, the United States also punched their ticket to the World Cup Round of 32 on Saturday, thanks to a 2-0 victory over Australia. Morocco and Brazil, who faced off in their opening match, both secured wins as well, edging past Scotland (1-0) and cruising by Haiti (3-0), respectively.

events.title: US-Iran ceasefire negociations underway in Switzerland
events.description: A ceasefire in Lebanon appears to have revived negotiations. US envoy Steve Witkoff flew out to join President Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner in Switzerland on Friday. They will meet Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghtchi. The US and Iran signed a memorandum earlier this week to stop the war, with implementation yet to be ironed out. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Iran, Reza Sayah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran ceasefire negociations underway in Switzerland

events.title: St Kilda pier wins peak Victorian architecture award as judges praise playful and ‘deeply civic’ design
events.description: State government project among range of works celebrated for community-centred design that goes beyond utility The reimagined St Kilda pier has added more accolades to its burgeoning trophy cabinet, taking out some of the top gongs at the 2026 Australian Institute of Architects’ Victorian awards. The $53m Victorian government project by Jackson Clements Burrows Architects, alongside Site Office Landscape Architecture and AW Maritime, took home the Victorian architecture medal on Friday, the award given to the most outstanding project of the year. events.title: Finland's parliament votes to lift decades-old ban on nuclear weapons in historic NATO defense shift

events.description: Trump announces U.S.-Iran MOU to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and address Iran's nuclear program while Iranian media claims victory over Washington.

events.title: France swelters under record-breaking temperatures
events.description: It's hot here in France. The country's weather service expects the current heatwave to be “widespread, prolonged and intense”, with temperatures in some areas potentially topping 40 degrees Celsius. Paris will still hold its Fete de la Musique celebrations, but local officials in other regions have cancelled the event.

events.title: Conflict over cattle in Brazil's Amazon highlights tensions for Indigenous peoples
events.description: In northern Brazil, a debate over cattle and conservation is unfolding on Bananal Island

events.title: Authorities find 4 bodies outside Mexico City while searching for a missing American
events.description: Mexican authorities were working Friday to identify four bodies found on the outskirts of Mexico City as part of the search efforts to locate a married couple who disappeared in May while living in Chicago and Mexico, a local prosecutor told The Associ...

events.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
events.description: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

events.title: India’s revolution in women’s cricket

events.title: Morocco fans dream of World Cup glory after Scotland win
events.description: This is what Morocco’s fans are saying after winning their first match at the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: Which teams have qualified for the World Cup 2026 knockouts, round of 32?
events.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage format, criteria and rules for qualification. Find out who's in and who's out.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
events.description: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

events.title: From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers
events.description: Fashion house pays tribute to Chinese style with its 75th anniversary catwalk show in Shanghai “New York may be the city that never sleeps, but Shanghai doesn’t even sit down.” For the British designer Ian Griffiths, who encountered this line in the New Yorker, it summed up why China’s biggest city was the right place to celebrate Max Mara’s 75th anniversary. “Max Mara is a product for metropolitan women, and it would be patronising to assume that a metropolitan wardrobe should be western-centric,” Griffiths said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers

events.title: Haiti and Türkiye eliminated, US through to knockouts
events.description: Team USA made it through to the knockout rounds thanks to their 2-0 win against Australia. Morocco quickly got a first goal past Scotland, as did Paraguay against Türkiye. Paraguay's Matias Glaraza scored 64 seconds in, a new 2026 World Cup record. Brazil finally got their first win of the tournament and powered three past Haiti in the first half. Tunisia is set to face off against Japan, in what is the 1000s World Cup game ever.

events.title: Canadian beer prices test German fans' thirst at FIFA World Cup
events.description: The FIFA World Cup is in full swing, including in Canada. Toronto is hosting Saturday's match between Germany and the Ivory Coast. The German supporters are there and they’re angry – according to them, the price of beer is far too high. Story by Daniel Quinlan and Antoine Fenaux.

events.title: UNESCO launches consultation on fair payment for news in the digital age
events.description: UN cultural agency UNESCO has launched a global consultation process to inform its Draft Guidance on Fair Compensation for News, particularly as online platforms and artificial intelligence (AI) increasingly rely on journalistic content.

events.title: Old and new challenges for the Human Rights Council as it turns 20
events.description: It’s 20 years to the day since the UN Human Rights Council began its work as the world’s principal forum tasked with promoting and defending fundamental rights everywhere, particularly the world’s most vulnerable people.

events.title: UN upholds freedom of movement for peacekeepers in Lebanon
events.description: The United Nations has again called for freedom of movement for its peacekeepers in Lebanon who continue to closely monitor developments in the south of the country, including in the wake of the recent provisional agreement signed by the United States and Iran.

events.title: Anti-G7 protest turns violent as demonstrators torch Tesla and smash UN office windows
events.description: Protesters set a Tesla vehicle on fire and smashed windows at a United Nations office in Geneva as they marched against an upcoming Group of Seven summit.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back

events.title: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’

The company said on Tuesday that Hidenori Furuta had stepped down after two years in the role. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’

events.title: Anthony Albanese says Australia’s first mainland case of deadly H5N1 bird flu ‘concerning’
events.description: Tests taken in Western Australia suggest a second bird was also infected, but there is no evidence poultry or agriculture systems are affected Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The deadly H5N1 bird flu strain has arrived on the Australian mainland with test results confirming a migratory seabird found on the Western Australian coast was positive for the disease. The agriculture minister, Julie Collins, confirmed a brown skua – found unwell last Sunday at Cape Le Grand national park near Esperance in southern WA had died from H5N1. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Anthony Albanese says Australia’s first mainland case of deadly H5N1 bird flu ‘concerning’

events.title: Iran says it's closing Strait of Hormuz again
events.description: Iran's joint military command said the strait had been closed due to Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon. Meanwhile, Iran's negotiators are heading to Switzerland for Iran-US talks on their interim deal. DW has more.

events.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
events.description: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

events.title: Poland's president strips Zelenskyy of top honor
events.description: Karol Nawrocki's decision to strip Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest honor is likely to spark a severe diplomatic crisis between Poland and Ukraine.

events.title: UK: Train collision north of London kills at least one person

events.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
events.description: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

events.title: 🔴 Live: Hormuz Strait closed over Israel attacks on Lebanon, Iran says

events.title: This small kamikaze helicopter defends Ukraine from Shahed drones
events.description: Russia’s long-range attacks on Ukraine usually involve a combination of drones, ballistic missiles and cruise missiles. The drones, mostly based on the Iranian ‘Shahed’ design, are getting faster, more sophisticated, and more numerous – but since last summer Ukraine has been deploying a powerful new weapon in defending against them: the interceptor drone. Our correspondent Gulliver Cragg spent a day with one of the teams using them to defend the skies over Eastern Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 This small kamikaze helicopter defends Ukraine from Shahed drones

events.title: Trump-Meloni row intensifies as leaders trade barbs
events.description: Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni has called out US President Donald Trump for "senseless", "constant, unprovoked attacks", as a row triggered by his claim she begged him for a picture escalates.

events.title: Death rate at camp in DR Congo spikes amid fears of Ebola spiralling
events.description: At least 30 people have died since the start of May in one camp for displaced citizens in the northeast of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), with some confirmed to have died from Ebola, in a sign the disease could be spreading quickly there.

events.title: Body found in South Carolina ID’d as missing fitness trainer who told friend she was 'scared for her life'

events.description: A North Carolina furniture manufacturer allegedly shot and killed his business partner hours after a court ordered him to pay $310,882.74 in a dispute.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Mainland Australia’s first suspected case of deadly H5N1 investigated after seabird tests positive for bird flu
events.description: Agriculture minister says no evidence of mass mortality or infection in poultry as WA authorities say a second bird is sick and being tested Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A migratory seabird found in southern Western Australia has tested positive for avian influenza with further testing under way to confirm if it is Australia’s first mainland case of the deadly H5N1 strain. The brown skua, a subantarctic species, was found at Cape Le Grand national park on Sunday and had since died, the WA government said on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mainland Australia’s first suspected case of deadly H5N1 investigated after seabird tests positive for bird flu

events.title: Labour's Andy Burnham wins a special election, setting up a showdown with Starmer to lead Britain
events.description: Labour's Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer for leadership of the country.

events.title: Rare Mozart manuscript discovered by French curator
events.description: A treasure has been found in France's national library: a notebook containing unpublished compositions signed by Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart from the time when he was giving music lessons. The pieces will be performed for the first time in public by two musicians from the Radio France France philharmonic orchestra as part of France's Fête de la Musique, a day dedicated to music which takes place every year on June 21. Story by Morgan Ayre.

events.title: Paint from Trump renovation peels off Washington's Reflecting Pool
events.description: The iconic reflecting pool, which stretches between Lincoln Memorial and the Washington Monument on DC's National Mall has turned green due to proliferation of algae in the water. It comes as the pool was recently reopened following renovation work ordered by Donald Trump that saw it painted American flag blue. Less than two weeks later, the paint is peeling off. Story by Camille Corcoran.

events.title: Police respond to reports of two trains colliding north of London

events.description: India's largest telecom operator is expected to raise around $4bn (£3.02bn), according to media reports.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly

events.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations

Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s son sentenced to four years in jail for seeking US interference in father’s Brazil coup trial

The office of Brazil’s ‌prosecutor general had ‌charged Eduardo Bolsonaro – who lives in the US - courting interference from the Trump administration to help Jair Bolsonaro’s case, by imposing sanctions on the court’s justices and tariffs on Brazilian goods. Continue reading...

events.title: Italy's Meloni, once Trump's closest ally in Europe, says he made up a story about her

events.description: Donald Trump's comments that Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni "begged for a picture" have elicited a furious response from Rome. Meloni said she was "stunned."

events.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
events.description: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

events.title: Lebanon: Is US-Iran peace deal a 'victory' for Hezbollah?
events.description: Lebanon's Hezbollah group has claimed that the US-Iran peace deal is a "great victory." The group, which is opposed to Israel, has certainly been bolstered by the deal. But what has Hezbollah really won?

events.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
events.description: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

events.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
events.description: China is driving the electric vehicle transition but Europe and other countries are catching up fast. EVs are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever.

events.title: Why are so many Brits in Paris for Fête de la Musique?
events.description: Block parties all night, live music till early in the morning, and... British tourists? In 2025, Paris's annual Fête de la Musique, the 'Festival of Music' marking the advent of summer, saw a wave of young Brits crossing the Channel to take part in the revelry. What's that trend all about? FRANCE 24's Tanishk Saha explains.

events.title: Trump unveils new converted Air Force One jet he accepted as gift from Qatar
events.description: Donald Trump has unveiled the new Air Force One, an upgraded Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar that has been converted into the official US presidential aircraft.

events.title: Producer who worked with Travis Scott, Drake and Beyonce dies aged 29
events.description: Grammy-nominated rap producer Tay Keith, who worked with artists including Beyonce, Travis Scott and Future, has died aged 29.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Producer who worked with Travis Scott, Drake and Beyonce dies aged 29

events.title: 'Completely made up': Italy's PM on Trump's comments she begged him for photo
events.description: Italian Prime ​Minister Giorgia Meloni has accused Donald Trump of making up a story about her, after ‌the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a picture with her at the G7 summit in France.

events.title: Canadian auto parts magnate Frank Stronach found guilty of sexual assault
events.description: Austrian-Canadian billionaire Frank Stronach has been found guilty of sexual assault and indecent assault

events.title: Top banker tried to sway Pope Leo XIV on rare earth mining
events.description: The head of Latin America’s top development bank has made a case to Pope Leo XIV about the potential of rare earth mining
events.ticker_label: 💥 Top banker tried to sway Pope Leo XIV on rare earth mining

events.title: Pope Leo XIV heads to northern Italy to honor St. Augustine and migrant saint Cabrini
events.description: Pope Leo XIV is traveling to northern Italy to honor St. Augustine and Mother Frances Cabrini

events.title: Cambodian Supreme Court upholds incitement conviction of opposition politician in charged case
events.description: Cambodia’s Supreme Court has upheld the incitement conviction of prominent opposition politician Rong Chhun but suspended the remainder of his prison sentence, allowing him to remain free while barring him from politics for years

events.title: Labour's Andy Burnham wins special election, setting up showdown to lead Britain
events.description: Labour’s Andy Burnham has won a special election for a seat in Parliament, positioning him to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer

events.title: For one young refugee in Uganda, basketball is more than a game
events.description: When 19-year-old Stephane Kulimushi looks around the basketball court in Kampala where he trains young refugees, he sees more than players.

events.title: Fermentation, flavour and the future of food: Making sustainability delicious
events.description: Words like “sustainability” and “healthy” shouldn’t take the joy out of eating. As elite chef Jaume Biarnés has been explaining to UN News, sustainable gastronomy can be delicious, exciting and fun.

events.title: Luigi Mangione overruled his own lawyers with a 'change of heart' on psych defense, legal expert says
events.description: Luigi Mangione withdrew his proposed psychiatric defense in the UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson murder case hours before a key court deadline.

events.title: FBI captures $1.2B Medicare fraud fugitive in Philippines, second arrest from Most Wanted Fraudsters list
events.description: Herbert Leon Kimble, accused of a $1.2 billion Medicare fraud scheme targeting the elderly, was captured in the Philippines and returned to the U.S.

events.title: Michigan trio allegedly beat, strangled and gagged man at Las Vegas hotel to collect on gambling debts
events.description: Three Michigan men allegedly kidnapped and beat a man at the MGM Grand in Las Vegas over gambling debts, demanding $185,000 from the victim's father.

events.title: Florida woman pleads guilty in scheme to sell thousands of fraudulent nursing diplomas
events.description: Carleen Noreus, 52, admitted to selling nearly 3,000 fake nursing diplomas through her two schools in South Florida. Her guilty plea came after a two-week-long trial.

events.title: Man accused of killing wife on wedding night brought back to US after years on the run in Mexico
events.description: Arnoldo Jimenez, allegedly accused of killing his wife on their wedding night in Illinois, has been returned to Chicago after a decade on the run.

events.title: Gilgo Beach serial killer's ex-wife says he 'got what he deserved' after he gets max sentence for 8 murders
events.description: Asa Ellerup says convicted Long Island serial killer Rex Heuermann "got what he deserved" after a judge sentenced him to life in prison for eight murders.

events.title: Switzerland confirms talks with the US and Iran are postponed and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Another lawyer joins Karmelo Anthony's appeal as killer claims he cannot pay for counsel
events.description: Karmelo Anthony secures new court-appointed lawyer to appeal his murder conviction for stabbing 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a Texas track meet.

events.title: Fitness trainer told friend she was 'scared for her life' weeks before body with matching clothing found
events.description: South Carolina fitness trainer Elena Moore told her best friend she was "scared for her life" weeks before a body matching her description was found.

events.title: Man charged with attempted murder, released after allegedly forcing toddler into crocodile enclosure at zoo
events.description: A man was charged with attempted murder after allegedly forcing a 3-year-old boy into a crocodile enclosure at a zoo in Huntingdon, England.

events.title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces potential leadership challenge from newly-elected Andy Burnham
events.description: Labour's Andy Burnham won a special election on Friday and gained a seat in Parliament. He has openly spoken about challenging Keir Starmer as leader.

events.title: From bear hugs to handshakes: How India lost its edge with Trump while Pakistan quietly gained ground
events.description: Amid India's push to isolate Pakistan, shifting geopolitics have elevated Islamabad's diplomatic profile. President Trump's meeting with Modi suggests New Delhi is looking to regain momentum.

events.title: Israel launches 'Biblical Highway' amid campaign to erase Jewish history
events.description: Israel's government brands Route 60 as the Biblical Highway, aiming to strengthen heritage tourism and the nation's identity as the land of the Bible.

events.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
events.description: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.
